response.justification: The candidate has a long and varied software development career, but fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, there is no evidence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a non-negotiable requirement. Additionally, the candidate has no demonstrable background in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or the security technologies specified (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). Their C/C++ exposure is limited to early-career work at National Instruments and a thesis project. The overwhelming majority of their experience is in web and backend development using TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across embedded, backend, and systems contexts
Early exposure to C/C++ systems programming at National Instruments and in low-level Linux audio work
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) indicating disciplined engineering habits
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned — a stated hard requirement that disqualifies the candidate outright
No experience with any of the required security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device authentication, or encryption protocols)
Predominantly web/backend-focused career with no embedded firmware, L2/L3 networking, or switch platform experience

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking background
